title: Ahaan: Software Process Intelligence: Mining Software Process Data for Extracting Actionable Information
authors: Sureka, Ashish; Kumar, Atul; Gupta, Shrinath

title: Agile Development with Software Process Mining
authors: Rubin, Vladimir; Lomazova, Irina; van der Aalst, Wil M. P.

title: Kanban and Process Mining in the Task Management
authors: Mendes, Vitor; Faria Junior, Elio Ribeiro; Garcia, Cleiton; Malucelli, Andreia
abstract: In a world continuously changing with markets that are in permanent evolution and in contend, the companys must inovate, be more flexible, offer quality and respond swiftly. These factors do not apply for product manufacture processes only, but for software development processes aswell. This paper describes an account of experiences with the use of Kanban to improve the activities management in an ERP's development. To do this, a mapping of the current process was carried out, so to propose a set of improvements in the management of activities. As a result, a set of tools were implemented to enable a clear view and control over the activities, as well as to integrate the Issue Tracking System with the Kanban board. Furthermore, a process mining technique was used to estimate the time of conclusion of the activities and to detect performance problem.
venue: Proceedings of the XVII Brazilian Symposium on Software Quality (SBQS '18)
ta_rationale: O paper atende IC1 (aplica process mining a artefatos de SW - Issue Tracking System e Kanban board em desenvolvimento de ERP) e IC3 (usa process mining para estimar tempo de conclusão de atividades e detectar problemas de performance em processos de desenvolvimento de software). Não há aplicação clara de critérios de exclusão.

title: Mining Software Development Process Variations
authors: Santos, Renata M. S.; Oliveira, Toacy C.; Abreu, Fernando Brito e
venue: Proceedings of the 30th Annual ACM Symposium on Applied Computing (SAC '15)
ta_rationale: Título fortemente indicativo de process mining em SW (IC1: 'Mining Software Development Process'), mas sem resumo disponível para confirmar se realmente aplica técnicas de PM (descoberta, conformance, event logs) ou apenas análise descritiva de variações de processo.

title: Modular Design Patterns for Systems that Learn and Reason
abstract: The combination of data-driven techniques from machine learning with symbolic techniques from knowledge representation is recognised as one of the grand challenges of modern AI. We propose a set of compositional design patterns to describe a large variety of systems that combine statistical techniques from machine learning with symbolic techniques from knowledge representation. As in other areas of computer science (knowledge engineering, software engineering, ontology engineering, process mining and others), such design patterns help to systematize the literature, clarify which combinations of techniques serve which purposes, and encourage re-use of software components. We have validated our set of compositional design patterns against a large body of recent literature.
venue: IEEE/WIC/ACM International Conference on Web Intelligence and Intelligent Agent Technology (WI-IAT '21)
ta_rationale: O artigo propõe padrões de design para sistemas que combinam machine learning com knowledge representation. Embora mencione 'process mining' como uma área relacionada, o foco principal é em padrões de design para sistemas de IA genéricos, não em aplicação de process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting de métricas em processos de desenvolvimento de software. Process mining é citado apenas como exemplar de aplicação de padrões de design, não como objeto de estudo.

title: Exploring the Impact of Situational Context: A Case Study of a Software Development Process for a Microservices Architecture
authors: O'Connor, Rory V.; Elger, Peter; Clarke, Paul M.
venue: Proceedings of the International Conference on Software and Systems Process (ICSSP '16)
ta_rationale: O título sugere foco em processo de desenvolvimento de software (microservices), mas sem resumo disponível não é possível confirmar se há aplicação de process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting. Pode ser um estudo de caso sobre processo de SW sem as técnicas específicas do escopo PATHCAST.

title: Mining Software Process Lines
venue: Proceedings of the 38th International Conference on Software Engineering Companion (ICSE '16)
ta_rationale: O título 'Mining Software Process Lines' sugere fortemente mineração de processos de software (IC1), mas sem resumo disponível não é possível confirmar se trata efetivamente de process mining aplicado a artefatos/logs de SW ou se é sobre software product lines. A ambiguidade terminológica ('process lines' pode referir-se a linhas de processos ou variabilidade de processos) requer verificação do conteúdo completo.

title: Understanding What Software Engineers Are Working on: The Work-Item Prediction Challenge
authors: Lämmel, Ralf; Kerber, Alvin; Praza, Liane
abstract: Understanding what a software engineer (a developer, an incident responder, a production engineer, etc.) is working on is a challenging problem - especially when considering the more complex software engineering workflows in software-intensive organizations: i) engineers rely on a multitude (perhaps hundreds) of loosely integrated tools; ii) engineers engage in concurrent and relatively long running workflows; ii) infrastructure (such as logging) is not fully aware of work items; iv) engineering processes (e.g., for incident response) are not explicitly modeled. In this paper, we explain the corresponding’ work-item prediction challenge’ on the grounds of representative scenarios, report on related efforts at Facebook, discuss some lessons learned, and review related work to call to arms to leverage, advance, and combine techniques from program comprehension, mining software repositories, process mining, and machine learning.
venue: Proceedings of the 28th International Conference on Program Comprehension (ICPC '20)
ta_rationale: O paper aborda predição de work items em processos de engenharia de software, combinando process mining, mining software repositories e machine learning para modelar fluxos de trabalho complexos em organizações de software. Atende claramente IC1 (process mining aplicado a artefatos de SW) e IC4 (mineração de repositórios para descobrir modelos de processo de desenvolvimento).

title: Fourth Annual Workshop on Data-Driven Knowledge Mobilization
authors: Noei, Ehsan; Lyons, Kelly; Stroulia, Eleni; Andritsos, Periklis
venue: Proceedings of the 29th Annual International Conference on Computer Science and Software Engineering (CASCON '19)
ta_rationale: Título genérico de workshop sem resumo disponível. 'Data-Driven Knowledge Mobilization' pode ser relevante a process mining em SW, mas sem abstract não é possível confirmar se atende aos critérios de inclusão ou se trata apenas de um evento editorial.

title: Understanding Student Interactions in Capstone Courses to Improve Learning Experiences
authors: Neyem, Andres; Diaz-Mosquera, Juan; Munoz-Gama, Jorge; Navon, Jaime

Não há evidência de que aplique process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting a processos de desenvolvimento de software, nem que minere repositórios de SW. O foco é em educação/pedagogia, não em processos de SW (EC1 - domínio fora de SW).

title: Process Mining Multiple Repositories for Software Defect Resolution from Control and Organizational Perspective
authors: Gupta, Monika; Sureka, Ashish; Padmanabhuni, Srinivas
venue: Proceedings of the 11th Working Conference on Mining Software Repositories (MSR 2014)
ta_rationale: Título fortemente sugestivo de IC1 (process mining aplicado a repositórios de SW para análise de defeitos), mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação do escopo exato, metodologia e se realmente aplica PM a artefatos de SW ou apenas analisa dados sem PM.

title: Monotone Precision and Recall Measures for Comparing Executions and Specifications of Dynamic Systems
authors: Polyvyanyy, Artem; Solti, Andreas; Weidlich, Matthias; Di Ciccio, Claudio; Mendling, Jan
abstract: The behavioural comparison of systems is an important concern of software engineering research. For example, the areas of specification discovery and specification mining are concerned with measuring the consistency between a collection of execution traces and a program specification. This problem is also tackled in process mining with the help of measures that describe the quality of a process specification automatically discovered from execution logs. Though various measures have been proposed, it was recently demonstrated that they neither fulfil essential properties, such as monotonicity, nor can they handle infinite behaviour. In this article, we address this research problem by introducing a new framework for the definition of behavioural quotients. We prove that corresponding quotients guarantee desired properties that existing measures have failed to support. We demonstrate the application of the quotients for capturing precision and recall measures between a collection of recorded executions and a system specification. We use a prototypical implementation of these measures to contrast their monotonic assessment with measures that have been defined in prior research.
venue: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology (TOSEM)
ta_rationale: O artigo aborda medidas de precisão e recall para comparação entre execution traces (event logs) e especificações de sistemas, aplicando diretamente a process mining (descoberta e conformance checking). Atende IC1 ao tratar analysis e quality assessment de process specifications a partir de execution logs, técnica central em process mining para software.

title: Mining Collaboration Patterns of Software Development Processes Based on Trace Alignment
venue: Proceedings of the 2017 International Conference on Software and System Process (ICSSP '17)
ta_rationale: Título indica aplicação de trace alignment (técnica de process mining) a processos de desenvolvimento de software, potencialmente satisfazendo IC1. Porém, ausência de resumo impede confirmação clara do escopo, metodologia e se efetivamente minera eventos/logs de processos de SW ou apenas padrões de colaboração.

title: Behavioral Petri Net Mining and Automated Analysis for Human-Computer Interaction Recommendations in Multi-Application Environments
abstract: Process Mining is a famous technique which is frequently applied to Software Development Processes, while being neglected in Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) recommendation applications. Organizations usually train employees to interact with required IT systems. Often, employees, or users in general, develop their own strategies for solving repetitive tasks and processes. However, organizations find it hard to detect whether employees interact efficiently with IT systems or not. Hence, we have developed a method which detects inefficient behavior assuming that at least one optimal HCI strategy is known. This method provides recommendations to gradually adapt users' behavior towards the optimal way of interaction considering satisfaction of users. Based on users' behavior logs tracked by a Java application suitable for multi-application and multi-instance environments, we demonstrate the applicability for a specific task in a common Windows environment utilizing realistic simulated behaviors of users.
venue: Proceedings of the ACM on Human-Computer Interaction (PACMHCI)
ta_rationale: Embora o artigo aplique process mining (Petri nets) a logs de comportamento, o foco é em recomendações de HCI e eficiência de interação de usuários com sistemas IT, não em processos de desenvolvimento de software. O domínio é HCI/usabilidade, não engenharia de software ou ciclo de desenvolvimento.

title: Clustering Software Project Components for Strategic Decisions and Building Reuse Libraries
authors: Srinivas, Chintakindi; Radhakrishna, Vangipuram; Rao, C. V. Guru

Pode ser apenas engenharia de software tradicional (reuso de código) sem foco em processos.

title: Uma Experiência em Mineração de Processos de Manutenção de Software
authors: Cruz, John Igor B. da; Ruiz, Duncan

title: Discovering Models of Software Processes from Event-Based Data
authors: Cook, Jonathan E.; Wolf, Alexander L.
venue: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology (TOSEM)
ta_rationale: O título é altamente relevante para process mining em SW ('Discovering Models of Software Processes from Event-Based Data'), sugerindo atendimento a IC1 e possivelmente IC4. Porém, sem acesso ao resumo e sendo de 1998, não é possível confirmar se a abordagem efetivamente aplica PM ou modelagem estocástica a artefatos reais de SW, ou se trata-se de método conceitual puro (EC3).

title: Software Development Process Mining: Discovery, Conformance Checking and Enhancement
authors: Caldeira, João; Abreu, Fernando Brito e
abstract: Software development has become a fundamental process on any business or organization. As a consequence, together with other emergent technologies, new development platforms (IDEs) are being created, mainly in the cloud (e.g., Eclipse Orion, Cloud9, Codio), requiring different approaches on the way software development can be studied. Empirical studies on software development most often are based on data taken from software configuration management repositories, source code management systems and issue tracking tools, but not from the IDEs themselves, because they do not record data publically regarding developers’ activities. We aim to bring forward new insights on the software development process by analyzing how developers use their IDE. Based upon process mining techniques such as process discovery and conformance checking, this missing perspective will hopefully allow the discovery of coding patterns, the search for programmer behaviors and the detection of deviations from prescribed processes. Finally, we expect to provide advice for individual software process enhancement.
venue: 2016 10th International Conference on the Quality of Information and Communications Technology (QUATIC)
keywords: Software development management; Process mining; Organizations; Codes; Programming; Soft sensors; Real-time systems; Capability maturity model; Big Data; Agile software development; software development; software process; process mining; pattern discovery; software engineering
ta_rationale: O paper aplica diretamente process mining (descoberta e conformance checking) a artefatos de desenvolvimento de software (IDE logs, comportamento de desenvolvedores). Atende claramente IC1 ao minerar processos de SW e descobrir padrões de codificação e desvios de processo.

title: Agile Development for Vulnerable Populations: Lessons Learned and Recommendations
abstract: In this paper we draw attention to the challenges of managing software projects for vulnerable populations, i.e., people potentially exposed to harm or not capable of protecting their own interests. The focus on human aspects, and particularly, the inclusion of human-centered approaches, has been a popular topic in the soft-ware engineering community. We argue, however, that current literature provides little understanding and guidance on how to approach these type of scenarios. Here, we shed some light on the topic by reporting on our experiences in developing innovative solutions for the residential care scenario, outlining potential issues and recommendations.
venue: 2018 IEEE/ACM 40th International Conference on Software Engineering: Software Engineering in Society (ICSE-SEIS)
keywords: Sociology; Statistics; Software; Software engineering; Collaboration; Tools; Stakeholders; Agile development; human centered design; vulnerable populations
ta_rationale: O paper foca em desafios de gerenciamento de projetos de software e aspectos humanos para populações vulneráveis, sem qualquer abordagem de process mining, modelagem estocástica, forecasting de métricas de processo ou análise de event logs. Não atende nenhum dos critérios de inclusão IC1-IC4.

title: Using Process Mining in Agile Software Development Methodologies: A Systematic Mapping Study
authors: Arias, Michael; Marques, Maíra R; Rojas, Eric
abstract: The structure and the way in which organizations manage their projects have evolved. Agile software development has emerged as an alternative to manage projects management processes more efficiently. Process mining allows the analysis of project historical information and proposing improvements for agile processes. A systematic mapping study (SMS) was conducted to classify the proposed approaches in agile development methodologies that uses process mining. A total of 502 studies were identified, and finally 6 studies were selected and analyzed according to distinct aspects. Conference proceedings is the most common venue. There is a concentration of approaches published that comes from Asia and Europe. Disco tool is the most frequently used tool. Meanwhile, the process discovery being the most relevant process mining type used by researchers in this research area. There are two evaluation methods reported as being used: case study and running example, where Scrum is the most frequently methodology used. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first research that has been conducted to generate a SMS in this research area.
venue: 2018 XLIV Latin American Computer Conference (CLEI)
keywords: Data mining; Software; Agile software development; Tools; Systematics; Business; Databases; Process mining; project management; agile development methodology; agile method; systematic mapping study
ta_rationale: O artigo é uma revisão secundária (SMS) sobre process mining em metodologias ágeis, não um estudo primário que aplica process mining a processos de SW. Embora o tema seja tangencial ao escopo (EC4 - revisão secundária fora do escopo direto), o paper não apresenta aplicação própria de process mining, modelagem estocástica, forecasting ou mineração de repositórios que atenderia aos IC.

title: Process Mining for Game Analytics
abstract: This paper explores the integration of process mining techniques and the User Behavior Mining (UBM) Framework within game analytics, using a expansive dataset from Age of Empires II. We demonstrate how these methodologies can be used to systematically analyze and interpret complex player behaviors across various skill levels. Employing techniques such as trace clustering and directly-follows-graphs, we identify distinct behavioral patterns and quantify differences in strategy adherence. Additionally, conformance checking reveals the impact of deviations from standard build orders on game outcomes. Our findings underscore the potential of PM and UBM as powerful diagnostic tools in game analytics, offering scalable and structured approaches to enhance player interaction understanding. The successful application of these methodologies not only introduces a scalable and structured approach to quantifying complex player dynamics but also opens up the entire range of process mining methodologies, algorithms, and software solutions available. This advancement enables the gaming industry to leverage sophisticated analytical tools previously confined to other domains.
venue: 2024 IEEE Conference on Games (CoG)
keywords: Process mining; Industries; Heuristic algorithms; Software algorithms; Games; Software; Standards; Process Mining; User Behavior Mining; Game Analytics; Player Behavior Analysis; Conformance Checking
ta_rationale: O paper aplica process mining a game analytics (Age of Empires II), não a processos de desenvolvimento de software. Embora use técnicas válidas de PM (trace clustering, directly-follows-graphs, conformance checking), o domínio de aplicação é comportamento de jogadores, não engenharia de software, atendendo claramente EC1.

title: Using process mining in software development process management: A case study
authors: Lemos, Artini M.; Sabino, Caio C.; Lima, Ricardo M. F.; Oliveira, Cesar A. L.
abstract: In this paper we describe the application of process mining techniques to analyze a software development process. Software engineering practitioners often conduct quality auditing of the development process to assure conformance with organizational standards. Despite some works have explored process mining techniques for the conformance analysis of general business processes, it is not of our knowledge any study that applies process mining to conformance checking of software development processes. Under a practical perspective, this paper explores a real database with event logs generated in the past five years of execution of a software development process. The database was gently provided by a Brazilian software house with annual revenue of more than US$ 500 million and includes more than 2,000 cases (process instances). The results show that process mining can be effectively employed as a supporting tool for the management of software development processes and for the improvement of the maturity level of software engineering organizations.
venue: 2011 IEEE International Conference on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics
keywords: Data mining; PROM; Programming; Process control; Software; Databases; Companies; process mining; software development process; conformance checking
ta_rationale: O paper aplica process mining (conformance checking) diretamente a artefatos e processos de desenvolvimento de software (IC1), com avaliação prática em event logs reais de 5 anos de uma organização de SW. Nenhum critério de exclusão se aplica.

title: Evaluating Coding Behavior in Software Development Processes: A Process Mining Approach
authors: Ardimento, Pasquale; Bernardi, Mario Luca; Cimitile, Marta; Maggi, Fabrizio Maria
abstract: Process mining is a family of techniques that aim at analyzing business process execution data recorded in event logs. Conformance checking is a branch of this discipline embracing approaches for verifying whether the behavior of a process, as recorded in a log, is in line with some expected behavior provided in the form of a process model. In the literature, process mining techniques have already been used to study software development processes starting from logs derived from version management systems or from document management systems. In this paper, we use conformance checking to test coding behaviors starting from event logs generated from IDE usage. Understanding how developers carry out coding activities and what hurdles they usually face should provide useful tips for improving and supporting software development processes. In particular, through conformance checking, we can compare different process executions, and identify behavioral similarities and differences. In our experimentation, we evaluated the activities performed by 40 novice developers performing coding activities in 5 development sessions. We assessed the developers to distinguish the ones obtaining the best performance. We then compared the behavior extracted from this group of developers with the others. The results show different IDE usage patterns for developers with different skills and performance.
venue: 2019 IEEE/ACM International Conference on Software and System Processes (ICSSP)
keywords: Encoding; Unified modeling language; Software; Data mining; Tools; Task analysis; Software Development Process; Process Mining; Conformance Checking; IDE Logging
ta_rationale: O artigo aplica process mining (conformance checking) diretamente a processos de desenvolvimento de software, extraindo event logs de IDE e analisando comportamentos de codificação de desenvolvedores. Atende claramente IC1 (process mining em artefatos de SW) sem violar critérios de exclusão.

title: Process Mining Perspectives in Software Engineering: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Jaqueline Urrea-Contreras, Silvia; Flores-Rios, Brenda L.; Angélica Astorga-Vargas, María; Ibarra-Esquer, Jorge E.
abstract: In the last decade, Process Mining has established as an emerging discipline for being oriented to the discovery, monitoring, and improvement of processes. This article presents the results of a Systematic Literature Review that reveals the state of the art of Process Mining perspectives in Software Engineering from 2010 to 2020, with the purpose of adequately characterizing software processes that are mostly complex. Twelve primary studies were selected and classified according to their type of publication, conceptual definition of Process Mining perspectives, their implementation in case studies and the successful contributions to the challenges and opportunities of implementing the perspectives identified in Software Engineering. The results identify four Process Mining perspectives (control- flow, organizational, case, and time), highlighting the use of the control-flow perspective in the cases of discovery of process models (flows and order of execution activities) and the organization- al perspective in the identification of human resource interaction. Although Process Mining perspectives have been applied in the Software Engineering domain, the lack of a standardized definition for the term Process Mining perspective and, in turn, for each of them, is still highlighted.
venue: 2021 Mexican International Conference on Computer Science (ENC)
keywords: Time-frequency analysis; Systematics; Bibliographies; Standards organizations; Process control; Organizations; Software; Process Mining; Software Engineering; Process Mining perspectives
ta_rationale: Este é um artigo de revisão secundária (SLR) que caracteriza o estado da arte de process mining em engenharia de software, não um estudo primário que aplica process mining a artefatos de SW ou contribui com novas descobertas sobre processos de desenvolvimento. Atende EC4 (revisão secundária fora do escopo da SLR PATHCAST), pois é uma revisão que caracteriza perspectivas genéricas de PM em SE sem foco específico em modelagem estocástica, forecasting ou mineração de repositórios.

title: Assessing Agile Software Development Processes with Process Mining: A Case Study
authors: Marques, Rita; Mira da Silva, Miguel; R. Ferreira, Diogo
abstract: Software development is a growing industry with increasingly more complex projects. Agile methodologies, such as Scrum, have emerged in part to address this challenge. Agile focuses on a set of best practices rather than a standardized process, which makes it difficult to assess whether it is being properly implemented in an organization. In this work, we analyze the feasibility of using process mining to evaluate the implementation of Scrum practices based on event logs collected from a case-handling system. A case study was conducted in an IT organization that uses Jira Software to manage its projects. With process mining, it was possible to extract the workflow behaviour in two different projects. While Scrum standards like role distribution could be discovered, other practices like customer collaboration could not be detected in the log. This case study provided important insights regarding the application of process mining in case-handling systems and Scrum processes.
venue: 2018 IEEE 20th Conference on Business Informatics (CBI)
keywords: Data mining; Software; Organizations; Scrum (Software development); Tools; Planning; Standards organizations; Agile software development; Scrum methodology; process mining; case-handling systems
ta_rationale: O artigo aplica process mining (descoberta de workflow, análise de event logs) diretamente a processos de desenvolvimento de software (Scrum/Agile), extraindo comportamentos de processo a partir de logs do Jira. Atende claramente IC1 (Process Mining em Software) sem violação de critérios de exclusão.

title: Applied process mining in software development
abstract: Current study uses the event process logs of a custom software development process used internally in the IT department of a large automotive company. The target is to extract hidden information about the process, difficult to identify due to the wideness and complexity of the input data. The log file contains all incident records tracked during one release of a software product. This analysis was performed using process mining techniques and tools included in ProM Framework, an academic project of the Eindhoven Technical University. The paper describes the steps followed to extract the working process model, organizational network and statistical information. Based on the obtained results, an action plan is put in practice with the scope of improving the development process and the release result.
venue: 2014 IEEE 9th IEEE International Symposium on Applied Computational Intelligence and Informatics (SACI)
keywords: Data mining; Software; Analytical models; Companies; Computational intelligence; Informatics; process mining; process model; event log; heuristic miner
ta_rationale: O artigo aplica explicitamente process mining (técnicas e ProM Framework) a event logs de um processo de desenvolvimento de software interno, extraindo modelos de processo, rede organizacional e informações estatísticas. Atende diretamente IC1 (Process Mining em Software) com dados reais de incidentes em um release.

title: Assessing Software Development Teams' Efficiency using Process Mining
authors: Caldeira, João; Brito e Abreu, Fernando; Reis, José; Cardoso, Jorge
abstract: Context. Improving the efficiency and effectiveness of software development projects implies understanding their actual process. Given the same requirements specification, different software development teams may follow different strategies and that may lead to inappropriate use of tools or non-optimized allocation of effort on spurious activities, non-aligned with the desired goals. However, due to its intangibility, the actual process followed by each developer or team is often a black box. Objective. The overall goal of this study is to improve the knowledge on how to measure efficiency in development teams where a great deal of variability may exist due to the human-factor. The main focus is on the discovery of the underlying processes and compare them in terms of efficiency and effectiveness. By doing so, we expect to reveal potentially hidden costs and risks, so that corrective actions may take place on a timely manner during the software project life cycle. Method. Several independent teams of Java programmers, using the Eclipse IDE, were assigned the same software quality task, related to code smells detection for identifying refactoring opportunities and the quality of the outcomes were assessed by independent experts. The events corresponding to the activity of each team upon the IDE, while performing the given task, were captured. Then, we used process mining techniques to discover development process models, evaluate their quality and compare variants against a reference model used as "best practice". Results. Teams whose process model was less complex, had the best outcomes and vice-versa. Comparing less complex process variants with the ""best practice"" process, showed that they were also the ones with less differences in the control-flow perspective, based on activities frequencies. We have also determined which teams were most efficient through process analysis. Conclusions. We confirmed that, even for a well-defined software development task, there may be a great deal of process variability due to the human factor. We were able to identify when developers were more or less focused in the essential tasks they were required to perform. Less focused teams had the more complex process models, due to the spurious / non-essential actions that were carried out. In other words, they were less efficient. Experts' opinion confirmed that those teams also were less effective in their expected delivery. We therefore concluded that a self-awareness of the performed process rendered by our approach, may be used to identify corrective actions that will improve process efficiency (less wasted effort) and may yield to better deliverables, i.e. improved process effectiveness.
venue: 2019 International Conference on Process Mining (ICPM)
keywords: Data mining; Task analysis; Tools; Java; Human factors; Software quality; software development; process mining; software process analytics; ide usage; efficiency assessment
ta_rationale: O paper aplica process mining (descoberta de modelos de processo) diretamente a artefatos de software development (eventos do IDE, atividades de desenvolvedores) para analisar e melhorar a eficiência de equipes de desenvolvimento. Atende claramente IC1 e o escopo da SLR ao descobrir processos reais de desenvolvimento de SW usando event logs e comparar variantes de processo.

title: Foundations for Event-Based Process Analysis in Heterogeneous Software Engineering Environments
authors: Sunindyo, Wikan Danar; Moser, Thomas; Winkler, Dietmar; Biffl, Stefan
abstract: For monitoring, controlling, and improving software development projects, project and quality managers need tool support to analyze engineering processes within development environments. Unfortunately, technical and semantic gaps between the engineering tools and related data models make it hard to observe and analyze the implemented tool-based engineering processes. In this paper we build on a service-oriented platform for technically and semantically integrating heterogeneous engineering tools and propose an approach to monitor, analyze, and improve tool-based engineering processes. We empirically evaluate the approach using the “continuous integration and test” process and discuss strengths and limitations. Major result was that the approach enabled comparing expected and real-life engineering processes with respect to process structure, performance of individual process steps, and risk of bottlenecks.
venue: 2010 36th EUROMICRO Conference on Software Engineering and Advanced Applications
keywords: Software; Analytical models; Semantics; Data models; Process control; Monitoring; Business; software process; software validation and verification
ta_rationale: O paper propõe uma abordagem para monitorar, analisar e melhorar processos de engenharia de software baseada em análise de eventos (event-based process analysis), aplicada a ambientes heterogêneos de desenvolvimento. Atende claramente IC1 (process mining em artefatos de SW, análise de event logs de processos de desenvolvimento) e IC4 (mineração de dados de ferramentas de engenharia para descobrir e melhorar modelos de processos de SW). Sem critérios de exclusão aplicáveis.

title: Detecting Bottleneck and Fraud in Agile Development by using Petri net Performance and Trace Clustering
authors: Razi, Muhammad Ar; Sungkono, Kelly Rossa; Sarno, Riyanarto; Wahyuni, Cahyaningtyas Sekar
abstract: Software Development Life Cycle model is one of the basic idea of the software development. SDLC is a continuous process, the cycle starts from the moment when it is decide to release the project, and it ends when its full remove from the exploitation. Agile is one of the mostly used SDLC models. Combination of incremental and iterative process models with a focus on process customer satisfaction and can adapt to rapid delivery of working sofware is called Agile SDLC. This contribution of this paper is evaluating the performance, such as occurring bottleneck and fraud, of an Agile model to handle the management of the project. This paper uses Petri net to analyze Agile SDLC model performance. The petri net use to detect the bottleneck that occur in the model. If an event has longer waiting time tahen others, it can identify that the bottleneck occur in that task. Then, the fraud is detected based on trace clustering. Based on the experiment, the cluster which has lowest member is indicates as fraud processes.
venue: 2019 International Seminar on Application for Technology of Information and Communication (iSemantic)
keywords: PROM; Data mining; Unified modeling language; Task analysis; Petri nets; Software; SDLC; Agile; bottleneck; alpha algorithm; Petri net
ta_rationale: O paper aplica Petri nets (modelagem estocástica) para analisar performance de processos de desenvolvimento Agile, detectando gargalos e anomalias através de análise de traces, o que satisfaz IC2 e IC1. Não há evidência de EC aplicáveis: o domínio é claramente SW, o software é o objeto de estudo (não apenas ferramenta), e há avaliação empírica em processos de desenvolvimento.

title: Identifying Software Process Management Challenges: Survey of Practitioners in a Large Global IT Company
authors: Gupta, Monika; Sureka, Ashish; Padmanabhuni, Srinivas; Asadullah, Allahbaksh Mohammedali
abstract: Process mining consists of mining event logs generated from business process execution supported by Information Systems (IS). Process mining of software repositories has diverse applications because vast data is generated during Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) and archived in IS such as Version Control System (VCS), Peer Code Review (PCR) System, Issue Tracking System (ITS), and mail archives. There is need to explore its applications on different repositories to aid managers in process management. We conduct two phase surveys and interviews with managers in a large, global, IT company. The first survey and in-person interviews identify the process challenges encountered by them that can be addressed by novel applications of process mining. We filter, group and abstract responses formulating 30 generic problem statements. On the basis of process mining type, we classify identified problems to eight categories such as control analysis, organizational analysis, conformance analysis, and preventive analysis. The second survey asks distinct participants the importance of solving identified problems. We calculate proposed Net Importance Metric (NIM) using 1262 ratings from 43 participants. Combined analysis of NIM and first survey responses reveals that the problems mentioned by few practitioners in first survey are considered important by majority in the second survey. We elaborate on possible solutions and challenges for most frequent and important problems. We believe solving these validated problems will help managers in improving project quality and productivity.
venue: 2015 IEEE/ACM 12th Working Conference on Mining Software Repositories
keywords: Data mining; Software; Process control; Interviews; Measurement; Companies; Process Mining; Qualitative Study; Software Development Life Cycle; Software Repositories
ta_rationale: O paper atende IC1 (Process Mining em Software) ao aplicar técnicas de process mining a artefatos de desenvolvimento de software (VCS, ITS, PCR systems) para identificar desafios de gerenciamento de processos. Também trata IC4 (Mineração de Repositórios para Processo) ao minerar repositórios para descobrir e melhorar modelos de processo de desenvolvimento. Não há violação clara de critérios de exclusão: o domínio é explicitamente software engineering, o foco é no processo (não em software como f

title: An AI-Enhanced Process Mining Framework for Software Process Insights
authors: Nguyen, Minh Khoi; Tran, Hanh Nhi; Abualsaud, Razan; Ober, Ileana
abstract: Using process mining to gain insight of software processes presents several challenges, especially in preparing good-quality input data for the mining algorithm from the low-level data recorded by non-process-aware systems, such as version control systems. This paper proposes a process mining framework integrating AI techniques to enhance and simplify the execution of process mining tasks in this context. First, we implement a learning process to build an ontology capturing the knowledge of the analyzed processes' domain. Then this domain ontology is used in constructing an event log that enables an effective process discovery and analysis. The domain ontology plays the role of a domain expert in matching low-level events to high-abstraction and meaningful process activities. We implemented and experienced the proposed framework to extract process knowledge of e-commerce web applications development.
venue: 2023 15th International Conference on Knowledge and Systems Engineering (KSE)
keywords: Knowledge engineering; Software algorithms; Process control; Training data; Reinforcement learning; Ontologies; Semisupervised learning; process mining; event log construction; domain ontology; ontology learning; process insight
ta_rationale: O paper apresenta um framework de process mining aplicado explicitamente a processos de desenvolvimento de software (e-commerce web applications development), extraindo event logs de version control systems e realizando process discovery. Atende claramente IC1 (process mining em artefatos de SW) e IC4 (mineração de repositórios para descoberta de processo de SW).

title: Applicability of Process Discovery Algorithms for Software Organizations
abstract: Process modeling is one of the most significant tasks software process improvement teams perform. Conventionally modeling is performed by teams composed of domain experts and process engineers and it takes considerable effort and time. In recent years there have been studies which use the data extracted from the actual events that took place to determine process models. In this paper we present the results of a case study we conducted to determine the effectiveness of four process discovery and process mining algorithms. After applying process discovery algorithms we compare the results by the actual process and process definitions. We discuss the discrepancies between the actual flow and the process definitions, and the weaknesses and strong aspects of the algorithms.
venue: 2009 35th Euromicro Conference on Software Engineering and Advanced Applications
keywords: Software algorithms; Data mining; Open source software; Software performance; Software tools; Software engineering; Application software; Informatics; Algorithm design and analysis; Programming; Process mining; process discovery
ta_rationale: O paper aplica explicitamente algoritmos de process discovery e process mining a processos de software (modelagem de processo em organizações de software), comparando modelos descobertos com processos reais. Atende claramente IC1 (process mining em artefatos/processos de SW) sem evidência de EC decisivos.

title: Layered Mixing Miner: A Process Mining Algorithm for Complex Programming Debugging Data
abstract: In the context of increasingly complex application scenarios and unstructured debugging data in software engineering education, analyzing students' programming and debugging processes presents significant challenges. This study introduces the Layered Mixed Miner (LMM), a novel process mining algorithm designed to address these challenges by generating inter-pretable models from complex and unstructured student programming and debugging data. The algorithm integrates various process mining techniques by constructing an initial process tree, calculating filtering thresholds, and employing diverse methods to construct process models based on different filtering results. Consistency tests demonstrate that LMM achieves higher accuracy and precision compared to established benchmarks, providing a reliable and insightful representation of debugging behaviors. Moreover, expert feedback further confirms the algorithm's superior interpretability and usability over baseline methods.
venue: 2024 7th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
keywords: Process mining; Accuracy; Filtering; Software algorithms; Education; Debugging; Reliability; Usability; Programming profession; Software engineering; Process mining; programming education; Educational Data Mining
ta_rationale: O artigo aplica process mining (algoritmo LMM) diretamente a dados de processos de software (debugging e programação de estudantes), extraindo modelos interpretáveis de event logs de comportamento. Atende IC1 ao aplicar técnicas de descoberta de processo a artefatos de engenharia de software.

title: Process Mining Model Integrated with Control Flow, Case, Organizational and Time Perspectives in a Software Development Project
authors: Urrea-Contreras, Silvia Jaqueline; Flores-Rios, Brenda L.; González-Navarro, Félix Fernando; Astorga-Vargas, María Angélica; Ibarra-Esquer, Jorge Eduardo; Pacheco, Iván A. García; Agüero, Carla L. Pacheco
abstract: This paper presents the mining of a software development project from the event logs stored in the software project management systems Issue Tracking System (Jira) and Version Control System (GitHub) to query commits. In addition, a private system owned by the small software development organization was also included that provides for the event streams stored in Jira and GitHub. The mined project implements the approach to integrated model covering the Process Mining perspectives to validate the execution of activities according to the very small software company's process definition, discover deviations and establish corrective actions based on the findings obtained through the control flow, organizational, case, and time perspectives.
venue: 2022 10th International Conference in Software Engineering Research and Innovation (CONISOFT)
keywords: Technological innovation; Process control; Project management; Organizations; Control systems; Software; Data mining; Process mining; Software engineering; Software development projects
ta_rationale: O paper aplica process mining (descoberta e conformance) a artefatos de software (Jira, GitHub) para analisar um projeto de desenvolvimento de software, cobrindo múltiplas perspectivas (controle de fluxo, organizacional, caso, tempo). Atende claramente IC1 e não apresenta critérios de exclusão.

title: Using Logs to Reduce the Impact of Process Variability and Dependence on Practitioners in Requirements Engineering for Traditional Business Process Automation Software
authors: Menezes, Thiago Medeiros de; Salgado, Ana Carolina
abstract: Context: Business Process Automation (BPA) is adopted by organizations to improve efficiency, reduce costs, and increase overall business performance. Traditional Business Process Automation (TBPA) is one of the main approaches employed to develop a BPA. TBPA entails the development of BPA in a programming language to integrate relevant applications in the digital ecosystem to execute a given process. Process variability and practitioner unavailability are issues that encumber the requirements engineering for TBPA software. Objective: This work proposes Requirements with Logs (RWL), a log-based approach for TBPA software to reduce the impact of these issues, by providing a higher alignment among business process requirements and software architecture, and employing process mining to semi-automatically discover the business process during requirements elicitation. Method: The research conducted a case study in a technology institute to assess RWL and report its results in practice. Results: The results revealed significant improvements in adaptability to business process changes, in time spent with practitioners, and in software development. RWL also presented limitations, including human intervention to accurately obtain the business process, complexity to trace the process into the architecture, data privacy concerns, and risk of network traffic overload. Conclusion: This research demonstrated the effectiveness of RWL to minimize the impact of process variability and the dependence on practitioners.
venue: IEEE Access
keywords: Business; Software; Automation; Process mining; Software development management; Organizations; Ecosystems; Requirements engineering; Software architecture; Costs; Process variability; practitioner unavailability; business process automation; requirements engineering; software architecture; software design; software development; software engineering
ta_rationale: O trabalho aplica process mining (descoberta de processos a partir de logs) explicitamente para análise de processos de software (requirements engineering para Business Process Automation), atendendo IC1. Embora o foco seja em BPA tradicional, a abordagem utiliza event logs e técnicas de process mining para melhorar a engenharia de requisitos de software, caracterizando claramente a intersecção entre PM e SE.

title: A User Evaluation of Process Discovery Algorithms in a Software Engineering Company
authors: Agostinelli, Simone; Maggi, Fabrizio Maria; Marrella, Andrea; Milani, Fredrik
abstract: Process mining methods allow analysts to use logs of historical executions of business processes in order to gain knowledge about the actual behavior of these processes. One of the most widely studied process mining operations is automated process discovery. An event log is taken as input by an automated process discovery method and produces a business process model as output that captures the control-flow relations between tasks that are described by the event log. In this setting, this paper provides a systematic comparative evaluation of existing implementations of automated process discovery methods with domain experts by using a real life event log extracted from an international software engineering company and four quality metrics: understandability, correctness, precision, and usefulness. The evaluation results highlight gaps and unexplored trade-offs in the field and allow researchers to improve the lacks in the automated process discovery methods in terms of usability of process discovery techniques in industry.
venue: 2019 IEEE 23rd International Enterprise Distributed Object Computing Conference (EDOC)
keywords: Companies; Systematics; Tools; Complexity theory; Software algorithms; Data mining; User Evaluation; Process Mining; Process Discovery; BPMN
ta_rationale: O artigo aplica process discovery (técnica central de process mining) a event logs reais extraídos de uma empresa de engenharia de software, avaliando a qualidade dos modelos de processo descobertos. Atende claramente IC1 ao aplicar process mining (descoberta automatizada de processos) a artefatos e contexto de desenvolvimento de software, com validação por especialistas do domínio.

title: Time Performance Evaluation of Agile Software Development
authors: Shani, Asadul Haq Muhammad; Sarno, Riyanarto; Sungkono, Kelly Rossa; Wahyuni, Cahyaningtyas Sekar
abstract: Agile is a method in software development that demand the development teams to work fast and adapt to changes that happens along the way. However, in the fact, there are activities in software development process that take longer time to complete than others, which is called bottleneck. Bottlenecks are difficult to find if there is no actual event log of the process. To provide the event log this research focus on creating an agile process model which can be executed and generate the event log. Besides the agile process model, this research also discover bottleneck not only from the whole processes but also from processes that are executed frequently. This research creates the agile process model using a workflow management systems. Then the bottleneck from the whole process and processes that are executed frequently are obtained by using Alpha ++ Algorithm and Heuristic Miner Algorithm respectively. The occurrence of activities over time is also analyzed in this research by using dotted chart analysis. This experiment proves that this research can create event log automatically and detect bottlenecks in whole process and processes that are executed frequently.
venue: 2019 International Seminar on Application for Technology of Information and Communication (iSemantic)
keywords: Heuristic algorithms; Petri nets; Software; Data mining; PROM; Business; Performance analysis; process mining; agile; bottleneck; alpha ++; heuristic miner algorithm
ta_rationale: O artigo aplica process mining (Alpha++ Algorithm e Heuristic Miner Algorithm) para descobrir e analisar processos de desenvolvimento de software (agile), gerando event logs e detectando bottlenecks. Atende IC1 (process mining em software) e IC4 (mineração de processos de SW para descoberta).

title: Process aware IoT: Semantic process mining in Event Logs
abstract: The emergence of the Internet of Things (IoT) has profoundly transformed the way companies manage their business processes by providing real-time information from a variety of wireless devices. However, integrating Internet of Things information into business processes presents major challenges due to the diversity and volume of the data. Our contributions were aimed at improving business process management in the context of the Internet of Things. In this particular context, a major obstacle to autonomous business process detection is the lack of modeling concepts that characterize Internet of Things components as part of a business process model. In previous studies, we investigated and compared process modeling in intelligent environments based on process mining to provide a framework for discovering business process models from sensor data. The usefulness of this method is associated with process modeling, which does not include IoT components such as devices and output data. In this work, we proposed an extended BPMN ontology model for the Internet of Things, which served as the basis for a semantic framework.
venue: 2025 9th International Conference on Management Engineering, Software Engineering and Service Sciences (ICMSS)
keywords: Process mining; Wireless sensor networks; Process modeling; Heuristic algorithms; Semantics; Ontologies; Data models; Internet of Things; Business; Context modeling; Internet of things; Process Mining; Event Log; Ontology; Semantic
ta_rationale: O paper foca em IoT e gestão genérica de processos de negócio (business process management), não em processos de desenvolvimento de software. Embora use process mining, não há evidência de aplicação a artefatos de SW (commits, issues, CI/CD, VCS, Jira) ou à engenharia de software especificamente.

title: A Mapping Study on Mining Software Process
authors: Dong, Liming; Liu, Bohan; Li, Zheng; Wu, Ou; Babar, Muhammad Ali; Xue, Bingbing
abstract: Background: Mining Software Process (MSP) helps distill important information about software process enactment from software data repositories. An increasing amount of research effort is being dedicated to MSP. These studies differ in various aspects (e.g., topics, data, and techniques) of MSP. Objective: We aim to study the state of the art on MSP from following aspects, i.e., research topics, data sources, data types, mining techniques, and mining tools. Method: We conducted a systematic mapping study on the research relevant to MSP at both microprocess and macroprocess levels. Results: Our mapping study identified 40 relevant studies that can be grouped into microprocess and macroprocess levels. The identified mining techniques have been mapped onto the associated mining tools that fall into four types. Driven by the three research questions which represented in a meta-model, the findings revealed the correlations among the research topics, data sources, data types, mining techniques, and mining tools. Conclusion: It is observed that in order to discover the software process model or map, the main data source is from industrial project. Current mining techniques for microprocess research are mostly business process mining or sequence mining techniques used to recover descriptive software process. In addition, various machine learning algorithms and novel proposed methods are used to improve the accuracy of macroprocess level factors (e.g., software effort estimation).
venue: 2017 24th Asia-Pacific Software Engineering Conference (APSEC)
keywords: Data mining; Software; Tools; Business; Manuals; Sensitivity; Software engineering; mapping study; software process; mining repository; software engineering
ta_rationale: Embora o paper trate de Mining Software Process e mencione técnicas relevantes, é uma revisão sistemática/mapping study secundária (EC4) que sintetiza o estado da arte em MSP, não um estudo primário que aplica process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting a processos de SW específicos. O escopo da SLR PATHCAST exclui surveys/SLRs que não tratam diretamente PM ou estocástico em SE como contribuição própria.

title: Process Mining and Automatic Process Discovery
abstract: Business Processes Modeling is essential for the management and execution of processes. However, when the execution of the processes differs from the pre-established models is necessary to review the traces and records of events to know these differences. One goal of process mining is to discover the real processes through the extraction of knowledge from the records of events available in the information systems. This paper describes a systematic literature review to identify the algorithms developed for automatic discovery of business processes. 20 articles that included algorithm proposals were identified and the results show that the algorithms provide similar models to the records of events when these events are clean without noise. In addition, it is observed that the most used technique to model the flows is Petri net.
venue: 2018 7th International Conference On Software Process Improvement (CIMPS)
keywords: Unified modeling language; Data mining; Petri nets; Software; Silicon compounds; Monitoring; Machine learning; process mining; event log; process model; discovery; techniques; algorithms
ta_rationale: Embora o artigo trate de process mining e descoberta automática de processos, é uma revisão sistemática secundária (SLR) sobre algoritmos de descoberta de processos em geral, não focada especificamente em processos de desenvolvimento de software. O resumo não menciona aplicações a artefatos de SW (commits, issues, CI/CD) nem a domínios de engenharia de software, atendendo assim o critério EC4.

title: A Conformance Checking System for Interaction Testing in Virtual Reality
authors: Vijay Aravynthan, S.R.; Reddy, Y. Raghu
abstract: The rapid growth of Virtual Reality (VR) across critical sectors like healthcare, education, and gaming necessitates robust methods for quality assurance. As VR applications increase in complexity, ensuring correctness of interactions becomes challenging. This paper presents our ongoing work on a novel, cross-platform conformance checking system designed to verify that VR interactions are as specified. Our system features three key parts: a JSON-based format for defining ideal interaction specifications, an intuitive visual editor for designing the interaction flows, and a rules engine that automatically compares runtime behavior against the predefined sequences. This system is designed to streamline the testing process and ensure behavioral consistency across diverse VR platforms.
venue: 2025 40th IEEE/ACM International Conference on Automated Software Engineering Workshops (ASEW)
keywords: Training; Process mining; Visualization; Quality assurance; Runtime; Virtual reality; Transforms; Stakeholders; Engines; Standards; Virtual Reality; Conformance Testing; Process Mining; Interaction Logging; Quality Assurance; Visual Editor
ta_rationale: O artigo aplica conformance checking a testes de interação em VR, não a processos de desenvolvimento de software. Embora use terminologia de process mining (conformance checking), o domínio é validação de aplicações VR, não análise de event logs ou modelos de processos de SW (commits, issues, CI/CD, lead time, etc.).

title: A Hybrid Approach to Process Mining: Finding Immediate Successors of a Process by Using From-To Chart
abstract: Process mining is a branch of data mining that aims to discover process model from the event logs. In this study, we propose a hybrid approach to process mining in such a way that, "from-to chart" is used as the frontend to monitor the transitions among activities of a realistic event log. Another novelty of this study is developed evaluation metrics, which are used for finding immediate successors in order to convert these raw relations into dependency/frequency graph.
venue: 2009 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications
keywords: Monitoring; Machine learning; Production; Software engineering; Neural networks; Noise measurement; Concurrent computing; Connectors; Petri nets; Machine learning algorithms; From-to Chart; Process Discovery; Immediate Successor; Process Mining; Event Logs
ta_rationale: O artigo descreve uma abordagem de process mining com descoberta de modelo a partir de event logs e métricas de avaliação para relações de dependência. Embora aborde process mining (IC1), o resumo não especifica claramente se a aplicação é em processos de software ou em domínios genéricos, criando ambiguidade sobre atendimento ao escopo da SLR. Não há evidência explícita de aplicação a artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD) ou repositórios.

title: Software Process Recovery: Recovering Process from Artifacts
abstract: Often stakeholders, such as developers, managers, or buyers, want to find out what software development processes are being followed within a software project. Their reasons include: CMM and ISO 9000 compliance, process validation, management, acquisitions, and business intelligence. Recovering the software development processes from an existing project is expensive if one must rely upon manual inspection of artifacts and interviews of developers and their managers. Researchers have suggested live observation and instrumentation of a project to allow for more measurement, but this is costly, invasive, and also requires a live running project. Instead, we propose an after the fact analysis: software process recovery. This approach analyzes version control systems, bug trackers and mailing list archives using a variety of supervised and unsupervised techniques from machine learning, topic analysis, natural language processing and statistics. We can combine all of these methods to recover process events that we map back to software development processes like the Unified Process. We can produce diagrams called Recovered Unified Process Views (RUPV) that are similar to the Unified Process diagram, a time-line of effort per parallel discipline occurring across time. We then validate these methods using case studies of multiple open source software systems.
venue: 2010 17th Working Conference on Reverse Engineering
keywords: Software; Programming; Data mining; Maintenance engineering; Documentation; Control systems; USA Councils; software development process; process; mining software repositories
ta_rationale: O artigo aplica técnicas de análise de artefatos de software (VCS, bug trackers, mailing lists) para recuperar e mapear processos de desenvolvimento de software, atendendo diretamente IC1 (process mining em software) e IC4 (mineração de repositórios para processo). Não se enquadra em nenhum critério de exclusão.

title: An application of process mining for queueing system in health service
abstract: Health services are looking for ways to improve the processes and optimize the service time. Process Mining has been applied in the process discovery in variety of domain. The process mining is a promising method to discover the activity behaviors. However, early applications of process mining do not support the queue analysis. In this work, we introduce the method for applying process mining with queue system for health services. Process mining was used for the process discovery and the queueing theory was used to model the performance. The experiment shows that the process mining discovered the actual process model and provided the information to construct a queueing model. The method is suitable for analyzing control-flow and time performance in health service domain.
venue: 2016 13th International Joint Conference on Computer Science and Software Engineering (JCSSE)
keywords: Data mining; Queueing analysis; Mathematical model; Process control; Drugs; Visualization; Computer science; Process Mining; Workflow Analysis; Queue system
ta_rationale: O artigo aplica process mining em domínio de saúde (health services), não em processos de desenvolvimento de software. Embora use técnicas relevantes (process mining + queueing theory), não há qualquer menção a SW, CI/CD, repositórios ou artefatos de desenvolvimento de software.

title: Process Mining of Duplicate Tasks: A Systematic Literature Review
abstract: Process mining improves and provides insights for business processes, which are information related to process execution. In general, process mining can be separated into three classes: process discovery, conformance checking and process enhancement. In order to simplify the process model, we make an assumption that both events in the log and tasks in the model have an injective relation in process mining, i.e., do not allow two tasks to share the same label (thus duplicates task). In addition, Duplicate tasks have some issues concerning the quality of process model discovered and the potential indeterminism in conformance checking. In this paper, we perform a systematic literature review of process discovery and conformance checking metrics for duplicate tasks. This review can: (1) provide a comprehensive review of the current work of duplicate tasks in process discovery and conformance checking; (2) help researchers choose proper process mining approach, tools, and metrics; (3) identify research opportunities in duplicate tasks.
venue: 2020 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Computer Applications (ICAICA)
keywords: Task analysis; Measurement; Data mining; Tools; Databases; Systematics; Bibliographies; process mining; duplicate tasks; systematic literature reviewe; process discovery; conformance checking
ta_rationale: O artigo é uma revisão sistemática genérica sobre process mining de tarefas duplicadas em processos de negócio, sem foco específico em processos de desenvolvimento de software. Não há evidência de aplicação a artefatos de SW, modelagem estocástica, forecasting de métricas de processo ou mineração de repositórios de software.

title: Identifying Bottlenecks of Hierarchical Process Model by Using Discrete Event Simulation and Process Mining
authors: Septiyanto, Abdullah Faqih; Sarno, Riyanarto; Sungkono, Kelly R.
abstract: Identification of bottlenecks is a challenge for researchers to detect and deal with as soon as possible so as not to affect the implementation of business processes in the actual situation. The complexity of business processes can make it difficult for analysts to identify bottlenecks in a business process. Process mining is a technique to detect bottlenecks using event logs. However, many studies still use event logs from the system resource activities. Therefore, identification cannot be made before implementation. The researcher proposes bottleneck detection using the Discrete Event Simulation (DES) hierarchical process model. The hierarchical process model is divided into Top-level and Bottom-level process models. DES is used to simulate a hierarchical process model that can generate event logs. The event log was analyzed using ProM with the Inductive Miner method and performance analysis. This research indicates that activities that occur bottlenecks at the Top-level can represent a group of activities that occur bottlenecks at the Bottom-level. Therefore, the bottleneck identification results in the hierarchical process model can provide an overview of bottlenecks in general (Top-level) and specifically (Bottom-level).
venue: 2022 IEEE 8th International Conference on Computing, Engineering and Design (ICCED)
keywords: Analytical models; Petri nets; PROM; Performance analysis; Discrete event simulation; Complexity theory; Optimization; Bottleneck; Business Process; Discrete Event Simulation; Inductive Miner; Process Mining
ta_rationale: O paper aborda process mining e simulação discreta para identificar gargalos, mas o domínio de aplicação não é explicitamente processos de desenvolvimento de software. O abstract menciona 'business processes' de forma genérica, sem evidência de aplicação a SW (commits, issues, CI/CD, repositórios). Embora use PM e DES (alinhados com escopo), falta vinculação clara ao contexto de processos de SW conforme IC1, IC2, IC3 ou IC4.

title: Improving Software Maintenance Using Process Mining and Predictive Analytics
authors: Gupta, Monika; Serebrenik, Alexander; Jalote, Pankaj
abstract: This research focuses on analyzing and improving maintenance process by exploring novel applications of process mining and predictive analytics. We analyze the software maintenance process by applying process mining on software repositories, and address the identified inefficiencies using predictive analytics. To drive our research, we engage with practitioners from large, global IT companies and emphasize on the practical usability of the proposed solution approaches, which are evaluated by conducting a series of case studies on open source and commercial projects.
venue: 2017 IEEE International Conference on Software Maintenance and Evolution (ICSME)
keywords: Data mining; Software maintenance; Companies; Maintenance engineering; Object recognition; Clocks; Process Mining; Qualitative Study; Machine Learning; IT Support Process; Bug Detection
ta_rationale: O paper aplica process mining em repositórios de software para analisar e melhorar o processo de manutenção de SW (IC1), e utiliza predictive analytics para abordar ineficiências identificadas (IC3). Não há indicadores de exclusão; o foco é explicitamente em processos de desenvolvimento/manutenção de software, não em domínios fora de SW.

title: Software process discovery using link analysis
abstract: Data mining is an exhausting yet very useful task. It can help programmers understand the nature of development process and reveal potential problems during the process. Thus more reliable software can be created. As open source software development communities thrive, many software repositories with useful information about the development process become publicly available, enabling us to do research. In this paper, the author explores an approach based on mapping the artifacts to the process activity of FreeBSD repository to study the control-flow of the development process.
venue: 2011 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Communication Software and Networks
keywords: Software; Educational institutions; Java; Birds
ta_rationale: O paper aplica descoberta de processos (process discovery) a repositórios de software de código aberto (FreeBSD), mapeando artefatos para atividades de processo e analisando o fluxo de controle de desenvolvimento. Atende claramente IC1 (process mining em artefatos de SW) e IC4 (mineração de repositórios para descobrir modelos de processo de SW).

title: Comparative Analysis of AI Software and Traditional Software GitHub Repositories Using Process Mining
abstract: This study conducts a comparative analysis of Artificial Intelligence (AI) software and traditional GitHub repositories, focusing on workflow efficiency and sentiment dynamics. Using process mining and sentiment analysis techniques, we examine repositories from eight prominent projects, encompassing diverse datasets of issues and pull requests filtered for relevance and consistency. Our findings reveal that AI software repositories exhibit different workflow patterns and sentiment dynamics compared to traditional repositories. Sentiment analysis uncovers that contributors to AI software repositories experience more positive sentiment dynamics, likely reflecting structured workflows and collaborative tools. Conversely, traditional repositories exhibit longer resolution times and more fluctuating sentiment patterns, which may indicate higher complexity or less automation. These insights provide valuable recommendations for optimizing repository management, fostering contributor satisfaction, and improving collaborative software development environments.
venue: 2025 20th Conference on Computer Science and Intelligence Systems (FedCSIS)
keywords: Process mining; Productivity; Sentiment analysis; Codes; Focusing; Software; Complexity theory; Iterative methods; Artificial intelligence; Software development management
ta_rationale: O paper aplica process mining a artefatos de software (issues e pull requests de repositórios GitHub) para descobrir padrões de workflow e eficiência em processos de desenvolvimento de software, atendendo claramente IC1 e IC4. Não há critérios de exclusão aplicáveis.

title: Applying data mining techniques in software development
abstract: Increasing complexity of software engineering and expansion of scope of application makes software credibility be greatly questioned. How to complete high-quality software production more efficiently is a new software development issue. Data mining has been applied widely in knowledge discovery; data mining technology being used in software development process can not only enhances the accuracy and comprehensiveness of software development but also enhances the credibility of the software.
venue: 2010 2nd IEEE International Conference on Information Management and Engineering
keywords: Data mining; Programming; Artificial intelligence; Software engineering; Application software; Production; Maintenance; Data analysis; Software testing; Computer science; software development; data mining; text mining; the degree of credibility
ta_rationale: O resumo é genérico e não apresenta evidências claras de aplicação de process mining, modelagem estocástica, forecasting de métricas de processo ou mineração de repositórios a artefatos de SW específicos. Trata-se de uma discussão conceitual sobre data mining em desenvolvimento de software sem metodologia, avaliação ou contribuição técnica identificável.

title: Outcome-Oriented Predictive Process Monitoring with Attention-Based Bidirectional LSTM Neural Networks
authors: Wang, Jiaojiao; Yu, Dongjin; Liu, Chengfei; Sun, Xiaoxiao
abstract: Outcome-oriented predictive process monitoring plays an important role for business process management, which is to predict the most likely outcome class (positive or negative) for an on-going case given the event log consisting of completed business process instances with labeled outcome classes. Unfortunately, the existing traditional machine learning methods for this problem require a lot of manual intervention and long time in real-time prediction applications. In order to construct a predictive classification model automatically, we propose an effective approach based on deep learning techniques, called Att-Bi-LSTM, which is combined with the bidirectional long short-term memory network and the attention-mechanism. This approach can capture and optimize the features that have a decisive effect on the outcome for a completed case so as to construct a predictive model with good performance. The extensive experiments conducted on twelve real datasets show that our approach outperforms the state-of-the-art ones in terms of prediction accuracy, earliness, and time performance.
venue: 2019 IEEE International Conference on Web Services (ICWS)
keywords: Business; Predictive models; Feature extraction; Encoding; Hidden Markov models; Neural networks; outcome prediction; predictive monitoring; business process; LSTM; attention
ta_rationale: O artigo aplica predictive process monitoring com deep learning em event logs, alinhado com IC3 (forecasting de métricas de processo). Porém, o resumo não especifica claramente se o domínio de aplicação são processos de desenvolvimento de software ou processos de negócio genéricos (BPM), o que é crítico para a SLR PATHCAST. Ausência de menção a processos de SW (commits, issues, CI/CD) ou repositórios de software gera incerteza quanto à aplicabilidade.

title: A hybrid approach for dynamic business process mining based on reconfigurable nets and event types
authors: Ning Li; Jianchu Kang; Weifeng Lv
abstract: Process mining aims at extracting business process information from event log generated by Business process management systems to analyze operational performance. It can be an interesting tool for actually measuring the alignment of business process. In this paper, the process mining for dynamic business is discussed. Among different instances of the same dynamic business process, there are often some structural and dynamic changes. A hybrid process mining approach to dynamic business process is proposed. The approach is based on reconfigurable net and event types. Reconfigurable net is used for modeling dynamic changes and two event types (start, over) are defined for mining algorithm. The method presented in this paper gives a method of mining dynamic changes in different instances and finally generate a reconfigurable workflow net
venue: IEEE International Conference on e-Business Engineering (ICEBE'05)
keywords: Aerodynamics; Data mining; Programming; Environmental management; Software development management; Information analysis; Performance analysis; Extraterrestrial measurements; Technology management; Monitoring
ta_rationale: O paper trata de process mining em processos de negócio genéricos (BPM systems), não especificamente em processos de desenvolvimento de software. Não há evidência de aplicação a artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD) ou repositórios de software, o que seria necessário para atender IC1 ou IC4.

title: Mining Business Processes: A Case Study of Healthcare Industry
authors: Kim, Jin-Hee; Hwang, Yoo-Joo; Hwang, Hyun-Seok
abstract: Process mining in healthcare involves analyzing event logs and data from various healthcare processes to gain insights into the sequential flow of activities, identify bottlenecks, and optimize operational efficiency. It enables healthcare organizations to visualize, monitor, and improve their processes for enhanced patient care and resource utilization.This paper explores the optimization of hospital processes, focusing on improving the patient transfer process through an analysis of patient transfer data. Variables include various aspects of the transfer process such as day, status, alert status, gender, age, personnel involved, locations, purpose, and times of different stages. Findings discover variations in transfer purposes across time frames, with specific categories exhibiting distinct average durations.
venue: 2025 9th International Conference on Management Engineering, Software Engineering and Service Sciences (ICMSS)
keywords: Process mining; Machine learning algorithms; Hospitals; Organizations; Predictive models; Personnel; Random forests; Optimization; Monitoring; Software engineering; Process Mining; Healthcare Business; In-hospital Transfer; Case Study
ta_rationale: O paper aplica process mining a processos de saúde (hospital, pacientes), não a processos de desenvolvimento de software. Atende EC1 (domínio fora de SW) de forma decisiva, mesmo que tecnicamente use técnicas de PM.

title: Causal Organizational Mining in Software Engineering: Evaluating Improvement Strategies in Development Team Dynamics
authors: Montoya, Fernando; Beltran, Cristian; Astudillo, Hernan
abstract: In the context of software engineering, analyzing causality in the dynamics of development teams is essential for optimizing performance. Causality involves understanding how certain factors (organizational structures, individual interactions) directly influence the team's performance, allowing for the identification and implementation of effective improvements in development processes.
